features.summary: Deere reported strong Q4 2021 results with net sales up 16% to $11.3B and equipment operations margin of 13.6%. Full year net sales increased 24% to $44B with net income of $5.96B. The company benefited from robust agricultural fundamentals, strong demand across segments, and successful price realization despite supply chain challenges and a UAW strike that ended in November.
features.management_outlook: Management forecasts fiscal 2022 net income of $6.5-7.0B with equipment operations cash flow of $6.0-6.5B. Production & Precision Ag segment sales expected up 20-25% with 20-21% operating margins, while Small Ag & Turf sales projected up 15-20% with 16-17% margins. The company expects demand to continue exceeding industry production capacity for a second consecutive year, with order books nearly full across most product lines.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing supply chain disruptions expected to persist through 2022, particularly semiconductor shortages and logistics constraints. The company faces $2B in material and freight cost headwinds, with 2/3 expected in the first half of 2022. Rising farmer input costs, especially fertilizer prices, could potentially impact equipment demand, though management believes precision technology adoption may offset this concern.
features.key_drivers: Strong agricultural fundamentals continue with robust commodity prices supporting farmer profitability and equipment replacement demand. Fleet aging and historically low new/used inventory levels are driving sustained replacement cycles. Technology adoption rates are accelerating significantly, with precision ag solutions like ExactApply and ExactEmerge seeing 55% take rates, while the company increases R&D investment by 17% focused on autonomy, electrification, and digital solutions.
